zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Margaret Beauchamp於1472年出生在英國英格蘭武斯特斯爾謝爾斯利 比奧甘時，她的父親Richard Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp38歲，母親Elizabeth Stafford48歲。她於1475年與John William Reade結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和8個女兒的父母。她去世於1503年3月27日在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾福朗格爾享年31歲。

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Beachum
meanings: Altered form of English   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: English:
possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Polish: variant of   Lada  .
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: ‘tendril’ or ‘creeper’, often used in metaphors describing the slender curvature of eyebrows, arms, hair, swords, lightning, and women's bodies.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Margaret Beauchamp was born in 1472, in Shelsley Beauchamp, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Richard Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp, was 38 and her mother, Elizabeth Stafford, was 48. She married John William Reade in 1475. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 8 daughters. She died on 27 March 1503, in Wrangle, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 31.

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Beauchamp(이)가 1472년 영국 잉글랜드 워스스터실르 셸슬리 보챔프에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp(은)는 38세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Stafford(은)는 48세였다. 그녀는 1475년 John William Reade(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 여덟 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1503년 3월 27일 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 랑글에서 31세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Margaret Beauchamp nasceu em 1472, em Shelsley Beauchamp, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Richard Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp, tinha 38 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Stafford, tinha 48 anos. Ela casou-se com John William Reade em 1475. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 8 filhas. Ela faleceu em 27 de março de 1503, em Wrangle, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 31 anos.

ru.St. Botolph Without Bishopsgate, London,: сеинт ботолф витоут бишопсгэит, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Margaret Beauchamp родилась в 1472 г. в местности шелсли бочамп, ворсестиршир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Richard Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Stafford, 48. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John William Reade в 1475 г. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 8 дочерей. Она умерла 27 марта 1503 г. в местности рангл, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 31 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady Margaret Beauchamp nació en 1472, en Shelsley Beauchamp, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Richard Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp, tenía 38 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Stafford, tenía 48 años. Se casó con John William Reade en 1475. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 8 hijas. Murió el 27 de marzo de 1503, en Wrangle, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 31 años.
